role: A collapsible orientation panel that explains the active lens and view state to the operator.
capabilities: Render a collapsible legend title and explanatory lines for the current projection and view mode
Show registry-declared binding meta descriptions when in the default un-centred, live state
Compute dynamic, centre-aware and time-aware descriptions for square, nucleation, separator, and semantic lenses
Describe the centred semantic strain field by iterating points to count inward/outward populations
Append the grid-and-circle spine note for the 'both' view on wheel lenses
Stamp the component with a ui://instrument/legend address
Provide accessible toggle button labels and expanded state
substance_why: It implements full conditional description logic for multiple lens types, collapse persistence, and rendering with accessibility attributes.
interactions_present: Clicking the toggle button collapses or expands the explanation and persists the choice
Legend content reacts to changes in projection, view mode, centre, and scrubbed time
interactions_missing: No keyboard shortcut or programmatic control for collapse
No reset to default expansion state
No per-line detailed help or tooltips
No cross-tab sync of the collapsed preference
No empty/loading placeholder when projection is absent
performance_risks: describe() and the centred semantic point-counting loop run on every render instead of being memoized
asOf() and array allocations for lines recreate values each render
Large proj.points arrays could make the strain-counting loop expensive
reuse_why: The lens-specific human-readable description rules are domain-specific and worth keeping, but should be extracted into memoized pure functions/hooks and decoupled from the presentational component.
decomposition_hints: Extract description computation into a pure function or hook (e.g., useLegendDescription) so it can be memoized and unit-tested
Extract the localStorage-backed collapse state into a reusable usePersistentToggle hook
Split lens-specific description strategies (square, nucleation, separator, semantic, both-view spine) into registry-driven or per-mode helpers
